authors_text: Teo Susnjak
title: ChatGPT: The End of Online Exam Integrity?
title_zh: ChatGPT：在线考试诚信的终结？
abstract: This study evaluated the ability of ChatGPT, a recently developed artificial intelligence (AI) agent, to perform high-level cognitive tasks and produce text that is indistinguishable from human-generated text. This capacity raises concerns about the potential use of ChatGPT as a tool for academic misconduct in online exams. The study found that ChatGPT is capable of exhibiting critical thinking skills and generating highly realistic text with minimal input, making it a potential threat to the integrity of online exams, particularly in tertiary education settings where such exams are becoming more prevalent. Returning to invigilated and oral exams could form part of the solution, while using advanced proctoring techniques and AI-text output detectors may be effective in addressing this issue, they are not likely to be foolproof solutions. Further research is needed to fully understand the implications of large language models like ChatGPT and to devise strategies for combating the risk of cheating using these tools. It is crucial for educators and institutions to be aware of the possibility of ChatGPT being used for cheating and to investigate measures to address it in order to maintain the fairness and validity of online exams for all students.
summary: This study evaluated the ability of ChatGPT, a recently developed artificial intelligence (AI) agent, to perform high-level cognitive tasks and produce text that is indistinguishable from human-generated text. This capacity raises concerns about the potential use of ChatGPT as a tool for academic misconduct in online exams. The study found that ChatGPT is capable of exhibiting critical thinking skills and generating highly realistic text with minimal input, making it a potential threat to the integrity of online exams, particularly in tertiary education settings where such exams are becoming more prevalent. Returning to invigilated and oral exams could form part of the solution, while using advanced proctoring techniques and AI-text output detectors may be effective in addressing this issue, they are not likely to be foolproof solutions. Further research is needed to fully understand the implications of large language models like ChatGPT and to devise strategies for combating the risk of cheating using these tools. It is crucial for educators and institutions to be aware of the possibility of ChatGPT being used for cheating and to investigate measures to address it in order to maintain the fairness and validity of online exams for all students.
abstract_zh: 这项研究评估了 ChatGPT（一种最近开发的人工智能 (AI) 代理）执行高级认知任务并生成与人类生成的文本没有区别的文本的能力。这种能力引发了人们对 ChatGPT 可能被用作在线考试中学术不端行为工具的担忧。研究发现，ChatGPT 能够展现批判性思维技能，并以最少的输入生成高度真实的文本，这使其对在线考试的完整性构成潜在威胁，特别是在此类考试变得越来越普遍的高等教育环境中。回归监考和口语考试可能是解决方案的一部分，虽然使用先进的监考技术和人工智能文本输出检测器可能会有效解决这个问题，但它们不太可能是万无一失的解决方案。需要进一步的研究来充分理解 ChatGPT 等大型语言模型的影响，并制定策略来应对使用这些工具作弊的风险。对于教育工作者和机构来说，认识到 ChatGPT 被用于作弊的可能性并研究解决措施以维护所有学生在线考试的公平性和有效性至关重要。

AI.tldr: 评估ChatGPT在在线考试中可能被用于作弊的能力及其对考试诚信的影响。
AI.motivation: 随着在线考试在高等教育中的普及，ChatGPT等AI工具可能被滥用，导致学术不端行为，需研究其潜在威胁并寻找解决方案。
AI.method: 通过测试ChatGPT执行高级认知任务和生成人类无法区分文本的能力，分析其在学术作弊中的潜在用途。
AI.result: 发现ChatGPT能展示批判性思维，生成高度逼真的文本，可能威胁在线考试的诚信，现有解决方案可能不够完善。
AI.conclusion: 需进一步研究大型语言模型的影响，教育机构应采取措施应对，以确保在线考试的公平性和有效性。

ai_content.motivation: 随着在线考试在高等教育中的普及，ChatGPT等AI工具可能被滥用，导致学术不端行为，需研究其潜在威胁并寻找解决方案。

authors_text: Soumya Sanyal,Yichong Xu,Shuohang Wang,Ziyi Yang,Reid Pryzant,Wenhao Yu,Chenguang Zhu,Xiang Ren
title: APOLLO: A Simple Approach for Adaptive Pretraining of Language Models for Logical Reasoning
title_zh: APOLLO：逻辑推理语言模型自适应预训练的简单方法
comment: Accepted at ACL 2023, code available at https://github.com/INK-USC/APOLLO
comments: Accepted at ACL 2023, code available at https://github.com/INK-USC/APOLLO
abstract: Logical reasoning of text is an important ability that requires understanding the information present in the text, their interconnections, and then reasoning through them to infer new conclusions. Prior works on improving the logical reasoning ability of language models require complex processing of training data (e.g., aligning symbolic knowledge to text), yielding task-specific data augmentation solutions that restrict the learning of general logical reasoning skills. In this work, we propose APOLLO, an adaptively pretrained language model that has improved logical reasoning abilities. We select a subset of Wikipedia, based on a set of logical inference keywords, for continued pretraining of a language model. We use two self-supervised loss functions: a modified masked language modeling loss where only specific parts-of-speech words, that would likely require more reasoning than basic language understanding, are masked, and a sentence-level classification loss that teaches the model to distinguish between entailment and contradiction types of sentences. The proposed training paradigm is both simple and independent of task formats. We demonstrate the effectiveness of APOLLO by comparing it with prior baselines on two logical reasoning datasets. APOLLO performs comparably on ReClor and outperforms baselines on LogiQA. The code base has been made publicly available.

The proposed training paradigm is both simple and independent of task formats. We demonstrate the effectiveness of APOLLO by comparing it with prior baselines on two logical reasoning datasets. APOLLO performs comparably on ReClor and outperforms baselines on LogiQA. The code base has been made publicly available.
abstract_zh: 文本的逻辑推理是一种重要的能力，需要理解文本中存在的信息及其相互联系，然后通过它们进行推理以推断出新的结论。先前关于提高语言模型的逻辑推理能力的工作需要对训练数据进行复杂的处理（例如，将符号知识与文本对齐），从而产生限制一般逻辑推理技能学习的特定于任务的数据增强解决方案。在这项工作中，我们提出了 APOLLO，一种自适应预训练语言模型，可以提高逻辑推理能力。我们根据一组逻辑推理关键字选择维基百科的子集，以继续对语言模型进行预训练。我们使用两个自监督损失函数：一种改进的屏蔽语言建模损失，其中仅屏蔽特定的词性单词，这可能需要比基本语言理解更多的推理；以及一个句子级分类损失，教会模型区分句子的蕴含和矛盾类型。所提出的训练范例既简单又独立于任务格式。我们通过将 APOLLO 与两个逻辑推理数据集上的先前基线进行比较来证明 APOLLO 的有效性。 APOLLO 在 ReClor 上的表现相当，在 LogiQA 上的表现优于基线。代码库已公开。

AI.tldr: 本文提出APOLLO方法，通过自适应预训练提升语言模型的逻辑推理能力。
AI.motivation: 现有逻辑推理增强方法依赖复杂的数据处理和任务特定的数据增强，限制了通用推理能力的学习。
AI.method: 基于逻辑推理关键词筛选维基百科子集进行继续预训练，采用修改后的掩码语言模型损失（针对特定词性）和句子级分类损失（区分蕴含与矛盾关系）。
AI.result: 在ReClor数据集上表现与基线相当，在LogiQA数据集上超越基线，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: APOLLO通过简单且与任务格式无关的训练范式，有效提升了语言模型的逻辑推理能力。

search_text: APOLLO: A Simple Approach for Adaptive Pretraining of Language Models for Logical Reasoning APOLLO：逻辑推理语言模型自适应预训练的简单方法 Logical reasoning of text is an important ability that requires understanding the information present in the text, their interconnections, and then reasoning through them to infer new conclusions. Prior works on improving the logical reasoning ability of language models require complex processing of training data (e.g., aligning symbolic knowledge to text), yielding task-specific data augmentation solutions that restrict the learning of general logical reasoning skills. In this work, we propose APOLLO, an adaptively pretrained language model that has improved logical reasoning abilities. We select a subset of Wikipedia, based on a set of logical inference keywords, for continued pretraining of a language model. We use two self-supervised loss functions: a modified masked language modeling loss where only specific parts-of-speech words, that would likely require more reasoning than basic language understanding, are masked, and a sentence-level classification loss that teaches the model to distinguish between entailment and contradiction types of sentences. The proposed training paradigm is both simple and independent of task formats. We demonstrate the effectiveness of APOLLO by comparing it with prior baselines on two logical reasoning datasets. APOLLO performs comparably on ReClor and outperforms baselines on LogiQA. The code base has been made publicly available. 文本的逻辑推理是一种重要的能力，需要理解文本中存在的信息及其相互联系，然后通过它们进行推理以推断出新的结论。先前关于提高语言模型的逻辑推理能力的工作需要对训练数据进行复杂的处理（例如，将符号知识与文本对齐），从而产生限制一般逻辑推理技能学习的特定于任务的数据增强解决方案。在这项工作中，我们提出了 APOLLO，一种自适应预训练语言模型，可以提高逻辑推理能力。我们根据一组逻辑推理关键字选择维基百科的子集，以继续对语言模型进行预训练。我们使用两个自监督损失函数：一种改进的屏蔽语言建模损失，其中仅屏蔽特定的词性单词，这可能需要比基本语言理解更多的推理；以及一个句子级分类损失，教会模型区分句子的蕴含和矛盾类型。所提出的训练范例既简单又独立于任务格式。我们通过将 APOLLO 与两个逻辑推理数据集上的先前基线进行比较来证明 APOLLO 的有效性。 APOLLO 在 ReClor 上的表现相当，在 LogiQA 上的表现优于基线。代码库已公开。 本文提出APOLLO方法，通过自适应预训练提升语言模型的逻辑推理能力。 Soumya Sanyal,Yichong Xu,Shuohang Wang,Ziyi Yang,Reid Pryzant,Wenhao Yu,Chenguang Zhu,Xiang Ren cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Guang Li,Ren Togo,Takahiro Ogawa,Miki Haseyama
title: Boosting Automatic COVID-19 Detection Performance with Self-Supervised Learning and Batch Knowledge Ensembling
title_zh: 通过自我监督学习和批量知识集成提高自动 COVID-19 检测性能
comment: Published as a journal paper at Elsevier CIBM
comments: Published as a journal paper at Elsevier CIBM
abstract: Problem: Detecting COVID-19 from chest X-Ray (CXR) images has become one of the fastest and easiest methods for detecting COVID-19. However, the existing methods usually use supervised transfer learning from natural images as a pretraining process. These methods do not consider the unique features of COVID-19 and the similar features between COVID-19 and other pneumonia. Aim: In this paper, we want to design a novel high-accuracy COVID-19 detection method that uses CXR images, which can consider the unique features of COVID-19 and the similar features between COVID-19 and other pneumonia. Methods: Our method consists of two phases. One is self-supervised learning-based pertaining; the other is batch knowledge ensembling-based fine-tuning. Self-supervised learning-based pretraining can learn distinguished representations from CXR images without manually annotated labels. On the other hand, batch knowledge ensembling-based fine-tuning can utilize category knowledge of images in a batch according to their visual feature similarities to improve detection performance. Unlike our previous implementation, we introduce batch knowledge ensembling into the fine-tuning phase, reducing the memory used in self-supervised learning and improving COVID-19 detection accuracy. Results: On two public COVID-19 CXR datasets, namely, a large dataset and an unbalanced dataset, our method exhibited promising COVID-19 detection performance. Our method maintains high detection accuracy even when annotated CXR training images are reduced significantly (e.g., using only 10% of the original dataset). In addition, our method is insensitive to changes in hyperparameters.

However, the existing methods usually use supervised transfer learning from natural images as a pretraining process. These methods do not consider the unique features of COVID-19 and the similar features between COVID-19 and other pneumonia. Aim: In this paper, we want to design a novel high-accuracy COVID-19 detection method that uses CXR images, which can consider the unique features of COVID-19 and the similar features between COVID-19 and other pneumonia. Methods: Our method consists of two phases. One is self-supervised learning-based pertaining; the other is batch knowledge ensembling-based fine-tuning. Self-supervised learning-based pretraining can learn distinguished representations from CXR images without manually annotated labels. On the other hand, batch knowledge ensembling-based fine-tuning can utilize category knowledge of images in a batch according to their visual feature similarities to improve detection performance. Unlike our previous implementation, we introduce batch knowledge ensembling into the fine-tuning phase, reducing the memory used in self-supervised learning and improving COVID-19 detection accuracy. Results: On two public COVID-19 CXR datasets, namely, a large dataset and an unbalanced dataset, our method exhibited promising COVID-19 detection performance. Our method maintains high detection accuracy even when annotated CXR training images are reduced significantly (e.g., using only 10% of the original dataset). In addition, our method is insensitive to changes in hyperparameters.
abstract_zh: 问题：从胸部 X 光 (CXR) 图像检测 COVID-19 已成为检测 COVID-19 最快、最简单的方法之一。然而，现有的方法通常使用自然图像的监督迁移学习作为预训练过程。这些方法没有考虑 COVID-19 的独特特征以及 COVID-19 与其他肺炎之间的相似特征。目的：在本文中，我们希望设计一种使用 CXR 图像的新型高精度 COVID-19 检测方法，该方法可以考虑到 COVID-19 的独特特征以及 COVID-19 与其他肺炎之间的相似特征。方法：我们的方法由两个阶段组成。一是基于自我监督学习；另一种是基于批量知识集成的微调。基于自监督学习的预训练可以从 CXR 图像中学习区分表示，而无需手动注释标签。另一方面，基于批量知识集成的微调可以根据批量图像的视觉特征相似性利用图像的类别知识来提高检测性能。与我们之前的实现不同，我们在微调阶段引入了批量知识集成，减少了自监督学习中使用的内存并提高了 COVID-19 检测的准确性。结果：在两个公共 COVID-19 CXR 数据集（即大型数据集和不平衡数据集）上，我们的方法表现出了良好的 COVID-19 检测性能。即使带注释的 CXR 训练图像显着减少（例如，仅使用原始数据集的 10%），我们的方法仍能保持较高的检测精度。此外，我们的方法对超参数的变化不敏感。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于自监督学习和批量知识集成的新型高精度COVID-19检测方法，利用胸部X光图像区分COVID-19与其他肺炎。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖自然图像的监督迁移学习，未充分考虑COVID-19的独特特征与其他肺炎的相似性，导致检测性能受限。
AI.method: 分两阶段：1) 自监督学习预训练，无需标注数据学习区分性特征；2) 批量知识集成微调，利用同批次图像的视觉相似性提升检测性能，优化内存使用。
AI.result: 在两个公开数据集上表现优异，训练数据减少至10%时仍保持高准确率，且对超参数变化不敏感。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过特征优化和知识集成，在数据效率和鲁棒性上优于现有技术，适用于实际医疗场景。

authors_text: Yingwen Fu,Wenjie Ou,Zhou Yu,Yue Lin
title: MIGA: A Unified Multi-task Generation Framework for Conversational Text-to-SQL
title_zh: MIGA：对话式文本到 SQL 的统一多任务生成框架
abstract: Conversational text-to-SQL is designed to translate multi-turn natural language questions into their corresponding SQL queries. Most state-of-the-art conversational text- to-SQL methods are incompatible with generative pre-trained language models (PLMs), such as T5. In this paper, we present a two-stage unified MultI-task Generation frAmework (MIGA) that leverages PLMs' ability to tackle conversational text-to-SQL. In the pre-training stage, MIGA first decomposes the main task into several related sub-tasks and then unifies them into the same sequence-to-sequence (Seq2Seq) paradigm with task-specific natural language prompts to boost the main task from multi-task training. Later in the fine-tuning stage, we propose four SQL perturbations to alleviate the error propagation problem. MIGA tends to achieve state-of-the-art performance on two benchmarks (SparC and CoSQL). We also provide extensive analyses and discussions to shed light on some new perspectives for conversational text-to-SQL.
summary: Conversational text-to-SQL is designed to translate multi-turn natural language questions into their corresponding SQL queries. Most state-of-the-art conversational text- to-SQL methods are incompatible with generative pre-trained language models (PLMs), such as T5. In this paper, we present a two-stage unified MultI-task Generation frAmework (MIGA) that leverages PLMs' ability to tackle conversational text-to-SQL. In the pre-training stage, MIGA first decomposes the main task into several related sub-tasks and then unifies them into the same sequence-to-sequence (Seq2Seq) paradigm with task-specific natural language prompts to boost the main task from multi-task training. Later in the fine-tuning stage, we propose four SQL perturbations to alleviate the error propagation problem. MIGA tends to achieve state-of-the-art performance on two benchmarks (SparC and CoSQL). We also provide extensive analyses and discussions to shed light on some new perspectives for conversational text-to-SQL.
abstract_zh: 对话式文本转 SQL 旨在将多轮自然语言问题转换为其相应的 SQL 查询。大多数最先进的会话式文本转 SQL 方法与生成式预训练语言模型 (PLM) 不兼容，例如 T5。在本文中，我们提出了一个两阶段统一多任务生成框架 (MIGA)，该框架利用 PLM 的能力来处理会话式文本到 SQL 的问题。在预训练阶段，MIGA 首先将主任务分解为多个相关的子任务，然后将它们统一到相同的序列到序列（Seq2Seq）范式中，并使用特定于任务的自然语言提示，以从多任务训练中提升主任务。后来在微调阶段，我们提出了四种 SQL 扰动来缓解错误传播问题。 MIGA 倾向于在两个基准测试（SparC 和 CoSQL）上实现最先进的性能。我们还提供了广泛的分析和讨论，以阐明对话式文本到 SQL 的一些新观点。

AI.tldr: 提出一种统一的多任务生成框架MIGA，用于解决对话式文本到SQL的问题，提升多轮对话中自然语言问题到SQL查询的转换效果。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以与生成式预训练语言模型（如T5）兼容，需设计统一框架以提升多任务学习的效率和效果。
AI.method: 采用两阶段方法，预训练阶段将主任务分解为子任务并统一为Seq2Seq范式，微调阶段引入四种SQL扰动以减少误差传播。
AI.result: 在SparC和CoSQL基准测试中达到最先进水平，并提供深入分析。
AI.conclusion: MIGA通过统一多任务学习和误差缓解策略，有效提升对话式文本到SQL的性能，为该领域提供新视角。

search_text: MIGA: A Unified Multi-task Generation Framework for Conversational Text-to-SQL MIGA：对话式文本到 SQL 的统一多任务生成框架 Conversational text-to-SQL is designed to translate multi-turn natural language questions into their corresponding SQL queries. Most state-of-the-art conversational text- to-SQL methods are incompatible with generative pre-trained language models (PLMs), such as T5. In this paper, we present a two-stage unified MultI-task Generation frAmework (MIGA) that leverages PLMs' ability to tackle conversational text-to-SQL. In the pre-training stage, MIGA first decomposes the main task into several related sub-tasks and then unifies them into the same sequence-to-sequence (Seq2Seq) paradigm with task-specific natural language prompts to boost the main task from multi-task training. Later in the fine-tuning stage, we propose four SQL perturbations to alleviate the error propagation problem. MIGA tends to achieve state-of-the-art performance on two benchmarks (SparC and CoSQL). We also provide extensive analyses and discussions to shed light on some new perspectives for conversational text-to-SQL. 对话式文本转 SQL 旨在将多轮自然语言问题转换为其相应的 SQL 查询。大多数最先进的会话式文本转 SQL 方法与生成式预训练语言模型 (PLM) 不兼容，例如 T5。在本文中，我们提出了一个两阶段统一多任务生成框架 (MIGA)，该框架利用 PLM 的能力来处理会话式文本到 SQL 的问题。在预训练阶段，MIGA 首先将主任务分解为多个相关的子任务，然后将它们统一到相同的序列到序列（Seq2Seq）范式中，并使用特定于任务的自然语言提示，以从多任务训练中提升主任务。后来在微调阶段，我们提出了四种 SQL 扰动来缓解错误传播问题。 MIGA 倾向于在两个基准测试（SparC 和 CoSQL）上实现最先进的性能。我们还提供了广泛的分析和讨论，以阐明对话式文本到 SQL 的一些新观点。 提出一种统一的多任务生成框架MIGA，用于解决对话式文本到SQL的问题，提升多轮对话中自然语言问题到SQL查询的转换效果。 Yingwen Fu,Wenjie Ou,Zhou Yu,Yue Lin cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Furkan Burak Bagci,Ahmet Alp Kindriroglu,Metehan Yalcin,Ufuk Uyan,Mahiye Uluyagmur Ozturk
title: Building Height Prediction with Instance Segmentation
title_zh: 通过实例分割进行建筑物高度预测
comment: Instance Segmentation, Satellite Images, Building Height Prediction
comments: Instance Segmentation, Satellite Images, Building Height Prediction
abstract: Extracting building heights from satellite images is an active research area used in many fields such as telecommunications, city planning, etc. Many studies utilize DSM (Digital Surface Models) generated with lidars or stereo images for this purpose. Predicting the height of the buildings using only RGB images is challenging due to the insufficient amount of data, low data quality, variations of building types, different angles of light and shadow, etc. In this study, we present an instance segmentation-based building height extraction method to predict building masks with their respective heights from a single RGB satellite image. We used satellite images with building height annotations of certain cities along with an open-source satellite dataset with the transfer learning approach. We reached, the bounding box mAP 59, the mask mAP 52.6, and the average accuracy value of 70% for buildings belonging to each height class in our test set.
summary: Extracting building heights from satellite images is an active research area used in many fields such as telecommunications, city planning, etc. Many studies utilize DSM (Digital Surface Models) generated with lidars or stereo images for this purpose. Predicting the height of the buildings using only RGB images is challenging due to the insufficient amount of data, low data quality, variations of building types, different angles of light and shadow, etc. In this study, we present an instance segmentation-based building height extraction method to predict building masks with their respective heights from a single RGB satellite image. We used satellite images with building height annotations of certain cities along with an open-source satellite dataset with the transfer learning approach. We reached, the bounding box mAP 59, the mask mAP 52.6, and the average accuracy value of 70% for buildings belonging to each height class in our test set.
abstract_zh: 从卫星图像中提取建筑物高度是电信、城市规划等许多领域中使用的一个活跃的研究领域。许多研究利用激光雷达或立体图像生成的 DSM（数字表面模型）来实现此目的。由于数据量不足、数据质量低、建筑物类型变化、光影角度不同等，仅使用 RGB 图像预测建筑物的高度具有挑战性。在本研究中，我们提出了一种基于实例分割的建筑物高度提取方法，从单个 RGB 卫星图像中预测建筑物掩模及其各自的高度。我们使用带有某些城市的建筑物高度注释的卫星图像以及采用迁移学习方法的开源卫星数据集。我们的测试集中属于每个高度类别的建筑物的边界框 mAP 为 59，掩模 mAP 为 52.6，平均准确度值为 70%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于实例分割的建筑物高度提取方法，仅使用单张RGB卫星图像预测建筑物的掩码及其对应高度。
AI.motivation: 传统方法依赖激光雷达或立体图像生成的数字表面模型（DSM）提取建筑物高度，但仅使用RGB图像面临数据量不足、质量低、光照阴影变化等挑战，需探索无需DSM的替代方案。
AI.method: 采用实例分割技术，结合带有建筑物高度标注的城市卫星图像与开源数据集，通过迁移学习训练模型，从单张RGB图像中同时预测建筑物掩码和对应高度。
AI.result: 在测试集上达到边界框mAP 59、掩码mAP 52.6，以及各高度类别建筑的平均准确率70%。
AI.conclusion: 该方法无需DSM即可实现建筑物高度预测，在RGB图像基础上通过实例分割有效提升了高度估计的准确性，为相关领域提供了新思路。

Predicting the height of the buildings using only RGB images is challenging due to the insufficient amount of data, low data quality, variations of building types, different angles of light and shadow, etc. In this study, we present an instance segmentation-based building height extraction method to predict building masks with their respective heights from a single RGB satellite image. We used satellite images with building height annotations of certain cities along with an open-source satellite dataset with the transfer learning approach. We reached, the bounding box mAP 59, the mask mAP 52.6, and the average accuracy value of 70% for buildings belonging to each height class in our test set. 从卫星图像中提取建筑物高度是电信、城市规划等许多领域中使用的一个活跃的研究领域。许多研究利用激光雷达或立体图像生成的 DSM（数字表面模型）来实现此目的。由于数据量不足、数据质量低、建筑物类型变化、光影角度不同等，仅使用 RGB 图像预测建筑物的高度具有挑战性。在本研究中，我们提出了一种基于实例分割的建筑物高度提取方法，从单个 RGB 卫星图像中预测建筑物掩模及其各自的高度。我们使用带有某些城市的建筑物高度注释的卫星图像以及采用迁移学习方法的开源卫星数据集。我们的测试集中属于每个高度类别的建筑物的边界框 mAP 为 59，掩模 mAP 为 52.6，平均准确度值为 70%。 本文提出了一种基于实例分割的建筑物高度提取方法，仅使用单张RGB卫星图像预测建筑物的掩码及其对应高度。 Furkan Burak Bagci,Ahmet Alp Kindriroglu,Metehan Yalcin,Ufuk Uyan,Mahiye Uluyagmur Ozturk cs.CV

authors_text: Guang Li,Ren Togo,Takahiro Ogawa,Miki Haseyama
title: COVID-19 Detection Based on Self-Supervised Transfer Learning Using Chest X-Ray Images
title_zh: 使用胸部 X 射线图像进行基于自监督迁移学习的 COVID-19 检测
comment: Published as a journal paper at Springer IJCARS
comments: Published as a journal paper at Springer IJCARS
abstract: Purpose: Considering several patients screened due to COVID-19 pandemic, computer-aided detection has strong potential in assisting clinical workflow efficiency and reducing the incidence of infections among radiologists and healthcare providers. Since many confirmed COVID-19 cases present radiological findings of pneumonia, radiologic examinations can be useful for fast detection. Therefore, chest radiography can be used to fast screen COVID-19 during the patient triage, thereby determining the priority of patient's care to help saturated medical facilities in a pandemic situation. Methods: In this paper, we propose a new learning scheme called self-supervised transfer learning for detecting COVID-19 from chest X-ray (CXR) images. We compared six self-supervised learning (SSL) methods (Cross, BYOL, SimSiam, SimCLR, PIRL-jigsaw, and PIRL-rotation) with the proposed method. Additionally, we compared six pretrained DCNNs (ResNet18, ResNet50, ResNet101, CheXNet, DenseNet201, and InceptionV3) with the proposed method. We provide quantitative evaluation on the largest open COVID-19 CXR dataset and qualitative results for visual inspection. Results: Our method achieved a harmonic mean (HM) score of 0.985, AUC of 0.999, and four-class accuracy of 0.953. We also used the visualization technique Grad-CAM++ to generate visual explanations of different classes of CXR images with the proposed method to increase the interpretability. Conclusions: Our method shows that the knowledge learned from natural images using transfer learning is beneficial for SSL of the CXR images and boosts the performance of representation learning for COVID-19 detection. Our method promises to reduce the incidence of infections among radiologists and healthcare providers.
summary: Purpose: Considering several patients screened due to COVID-19 pandemic, computer-aided detection has strong potential in assisting clinical workflow efficiency and reducing the incidence of infections among radiologists and healthcare providers. Since many confirmed COVID-19 cases present radiological findings of pneumonia, radiologic examinations can be useful for fast detection. Therefore, chest radiography can be used to fast screen COVID-19 during the patient triage, thereby determining the priority of patient's care to help saturated medical facilities in a pandemic situation. Methods: In this paper, we propose a new learning scheme called self-supervised transfer learning for detecting COVID-19 from chest X-ray (CXR) images. We compared six self-supervised learning (SSL) methods (Cross, BYOL, SimSiam, SimCLR, PIRL-jigsaw, and PIRL-rotation) with the proposed method. Additionally, we compared six pretrained DCNNs (ResNet18, ResNet50, ResNet101, CheXNet, DenseNet201, and InceptionV3) with the proposed method. We provide quantitative evaluation on the largest open COVID-19 CXR dataset and qualitative results for visual inspection. Results: Our method achieved a harmonic mean (HM) score of 0.985, AUC of 0.999, and four-class accuracy of 0.953. We also used the visualization technique Grad-CAM++ to generate visual explanations of different classes of CXR images with the proposed method to increase the interpretability. Conclusions: Our method shows that the knowledge learned from natural images using transfer learning is beneficial for SSL of the CXR images and boosts the performance of representation learning for COVID-19 detection. Our method promises to reduce the incidence of infections among radiologists and healthcare providers.
abstract_zh: 目的：考虑到因 COVID-19 大流行而筛查的几名患者，计算机辅助检测在提高临床工作流程效率并降低放射科医生和医疗保健提供者的感染发生率方面具有巨大潜力。由于许多确诊的 COVID-19 病例出现肺炎的放射学表现，放射学检查可有助于快速检测。因此，胸部X光检查可用于在患者分诊过程中快速筛查COVID-19，从而确定患者护理的优先顺序，以帮助大流行情况下饱和的医疗设施。方法：在本文中，我们提出了一种称为自监督迁移学习的新学习方案，用于从胸部 X 射线 (CXR) 图像中检测 COVID-19。我们将六种自监督学习 (SSL) 方法（Cross、BYOL、SimSiam、SimCLR、PIRL-jigsaw 和 PIRL-rotation）与所提出的方法进行了比较。此外，我们还将六个预训练的 DCNN（ResNet18、ResNet50、ResNet101、CheXNet、DenseNet201 和 InceptionV3）与所提出的方法进行了比较。我们提供对最大的开放式 COVID-19 CXR 数据集的定量评估和目视检查的定性结果。结果：我们的方法实现了 0.985 的调和平均 (HM) 分数、0.999 的 AUC 和 0.953 的四级准确度。我们还使用可视化技术 Grad-CAM++ 通过所提出的方法生成不同类别 CXR 图像的视觉解释，以提高可解释性。结论：我们的方法表明，使用迁移学习从自然图像中学习的知识有利于 CXR 图像的 SSL，并提高了 COVID-19 检测的表示学习的性能。我们的方法有望降低放射科医生和医疗保健提供者的感染发生率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于自监督迁移学习的新冠肺炎检测方法，利用胸部X光图像进行快速筛查。
AI.motivation: 疫情期间需提高临床筛查效率并减少医护人员感染风险，胸部X光能快速发现肺炎特征，适合用于患者分诊。
AI.method: 提出自监督迁移学习框架，对比六种自监督学习方法和六种预训练深度卷积神经网络，并使用Grad-CAM++进行可视化解释。
AI.result: 方法在最大公开数据集上达到0.985的调和平均值、0.999的AUC和0.953的四分类准确率，可视化结果增强模型可解释性。
AI.conclusion: 从自然图像迁移的知识可提升胸部X光的自监督学习效果，该方法有望降低医护人员感染风险并提升检测性能。

search_text: COVID-19 Detection Based on Self-Supervised Transfer Learning Using Chest X-Ray Images 使用胸部 X 射线图像进行基于自监督迁移学习的 COVID-19 检测 Purpose: Considering several patients screened due to COVID-19 pandemic, computer-aided detection has strong potential in assisting clinical workflow efficiency and reducing the incidence of infections among radiologists and healthcare providers. Since many confirmed COVID-19 cases present radiological findings of pneumonia, radiologic examinations can be useful for fast detection. Therefore, chest radiography can be used to fast screen COVID-19 during the patient triage, thereby determining the priority of patient's care to help saturated medical facilities in a pandemic situation. Methods: In this paper, we propose a new learning scheme called self-supervised transfer learning for detecting COVID-19 from chest X-ray (CXR) images. We compared six self-supervised learning (SSL) methods (Cross, BYOL, SimSiam, SimCLR, PIRL-jigsaw, and PIRL-rotation) with the proposed method. Additionally, we compared six pretrained DCNNs (ResNet18, ResNet50, ResNet101, CheXNet, DenseNet201, and InceptionV3) with the proposed method. We provide quantitative evaluation on the largest open COVID-19 CXR dataset and qualitative results for visual inspection. Results: Our method achieved a harmonic mean (HM) score of 0.985, AUC of 0.999, and four-class accuracy of 0.953. We also used the visualization technique Grad-CAM++ to generate visual explanations of different classes of CXR images with the proposed method to increase the interpretability. Conclusions: Our method shows that the knowledge learned from natural images using transfer learning is beneficial for SSL of the CXR images and boosts the performance of representation learning for COVID-19 detection. Our method promises to reduce the incidence of infections among radiologists and healthcare providers. 目的：考虑到因 COVID-19 大流行而筛查的几名患者，计算机辅助检测在提高临床工作流程效率并降低放射科医生和医疗保健提供者的感染发生率方面具有巨大潜力。由于许多确诊的 COVID-19 病例出现肺炎的放射学表现，放射学检查可有助于快速检测。因此，胸部X光检查可用于在患者分诊过程中快速筛查COVID-19，从而确定患者护理的优先顺序，以帮助大流行情况下饱和的医疗设施。方法：在本文中，我们提出了一种称为自监督迁移学习的新学习方案，用于从胸部 X 射线 (CXR) 图像中检测 COVID-19。我们将六种自监督学习 (SSL) 方法（Cross、BYOL、SimSiam、SimCLR、PIRL-jigsaw 和 PIRL-rotation）与所提出的方法进行了比较。此外，我们还将六个预训练的 DCNN（ResNet18、ResNet50、ResNet101、CheXNet、DenseNet201 和 InceptionV3）与所提出的方法进行了比较。我们提供对最大的开放式 COVID-19 CXR 数据集的定量评估和目视检查的定性结果。结果：我们的方法实现了 0.985 的调和平均 (HM) 分数、0.999 的 AUC 和 0.953 的四级准确度。我们还使用可视化技术 Grad-CAM++ 通过所提出的方法生成不同类别 CXR 图像的视觉解释，以提高可解释性。结论：我们的方法表明，使用迁移学习从自然图像中学习的知识有利于 CXR 图像的 SSL，并提高了 COVID-19 检测的表示学习的性能。我们的方法有望降低放射科医生和医疗保健提供者的感染发生率。 本文提出了一种基于自监督迁移学习的新冠肺炎检测方法，利用胸部X光图像进行快速筛查。 Guang Li,Ren Togo,Takahiro Ogawa,Miki Haseyama eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Cheng-Ju Ho,Chen-Hsuan Tai,Yi-Hsuan Tsai,Yen-Yu Lin,Ming-Hsuan Yang
title: Learning Object-level Point Augmentor for Semi-supervised 3D Object Detection
title_zh: 用于半监督 3D 对象检测的学习对象级点增强器
abstract: Semi-supervised object detection is important for 3D scene understanding because obtaining large-scale 3D bounding box annotations on point clouds is time-consuming and labor-intensive. Existing semi-supervised methods usually employ teacher-student knowledge distillation together with an augmentation strategy to leverage unlabeled point clouds. However, these methods adopt global augmentation with scene-level transformations and hence are sub-optimal for instance-level object detection. In this work, we propose an object-level point augmentor (OPA) that performs local transformations for semi-supervised 3D object detection. In this way, the resultant augmentor is derived to emphasize object instances rather than irrelevant backgrounds, making the augmented data more useful for object detector training. Extensive experiments on the ScanNet and SUN RGB-D datasets show that the proposed OPA performs favorably against the state-of-the-art methods under various experimental settings. The source code will be available at https://github.com/nomiaro/OPA.
summary: Semi-supervised object detection is important for 3D scene understanding because obtaining large-scale 3D bounding box annotations on point clouds is time-consuming and labor-intensive. Existing semi-supervised methods usually employ teacher-student knowledge distillation together with an augmentation strategy to leverage unlabeled point clouds. However, these methods adopt global augmentation with scene-level transformations and hence are sub-optimal for instance-level object detection. In this work, we propose an object-level point augmentor (OPA) that performs local transformations for semi-supervised 3D object detection. In this way, the resultant augmentor is derived to emphasize object instances rather than irrelevant backgrounds, making the augmented data more useful for object detector training. Extensive experiments on the ScanNet and SUN RGB-D datasets show that the proposed OPA performs favorably against the state-of-the-art methods under various experimental settings. The source code will be available at https://github.com/nomiaro/OPA.
abstract_zh: 半监督对象检测对于 3D 场景理解非常重要，因为在点云上获取大规模 3D 边界框注释既耗时又费力。现有的半监督方法通常采用师生知识蒸馏和增强策略来利用未标记的点云。然而，这些方法采用场景级变换的全局增强，因此对于实例级对象检测来说不是最优的。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种对象级点增强器 (OPA)，它为半监督 3D 对象检测执行局部变换。通过这种方式，得到的增强器被导出来强调对象实例而不是不相关的背景，使得增强数据对于对象检测器训练更有用。对 ScanNet 和 SUN RGB-D 数据集的大量实验表明，所提出的 OPA 在各种实验设置下的性能优于最先进的方法。源代码可在 https://github.com/nomiaro/OPA 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种对象级点增强方法（OPA），用于改进半监督3D目标检测中未标记点云的数据增强策略。
AI.motivation: 现有半监督方法采用全局场景级增强策略，无法有效提升实例级目标检测性能，导致未标记数据利用率不足。
AI.method: 设计对象级点增强器（OPA），通过局部几何变换聚焦目标实例区域，生成更符合检测任务需求的增强样本。
AI.result: 在ScanNet和SUN RGB-D数据集上，OPA在多种实验设置下均优于现有最先进方法，验证了方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: 局部对象级增强策略能显著提升半监督3D目标检测性能，为未标记点云的利用提供了新思路。

authors_text: Meng Jiang
title: Very Large Language Model as a Unified Methodology of Text Mining
title_zh: 超大型语言模型作为文本挖掘的统一方法
abstract: Text data mining is the process of deriving essential information from language text. Typical text mining tasks include text categorization, text clustering, topic modeling, information extraction, and text summarization. Various data sets are collected and various algorithms are designed for the different types of tasks. In this paper, I present a blue sky idea that very large language model (VLLM) will become an effective unified methodology of text mining. I discuss at least three advantages of this new methodology against conventional methods. Finally I discuss the challenges in the design and development of VLLM techniques for text mining.
summary: Text data mining is the process of deriving essential information from language text. Typical text mining tasks include text categorization, text clustering, topic modeling, information extraction, and text summarization. Various data sets are collected and various algorithms are designed for the different types of tasks. In this paper, I present a blue sky idea that very large language model (VLLM) will become an effective unified methodology of text mining. I discuss at least three advantages of this new methodology against conventional methods. Finally I discuss the challenges in the design and development of VLLM techniques for text mining.
abstract_zh: 文本数据挖掘是从语言文本中获取基本信息的过程。典型的文本挖掘任务包括文本分类、文本聚类、主题建模、信息提取和文本摘要。收集各种数据集并为不同类型的任务设计各种算法。在本文中，我提出了一个蓝天想法，即超大型语言模型（VLLM）将成为文本挖掘的有效统一方法。我至少讨论了这种新方法相对于传统方法的三个优点。最后我讨论了用于文本挖掘的 VLLM 技术的设计和开发所面临的挑战。

AI.tldr: 提出将超大规模语言模型作为文本挖掘的统一方法论，探讨其优势与挑战
AI.motivation: 传统文本挖掘方法需针对不同任务设计不同算法和数据集，效率低且复杂，需更统一的方法
AI.method: 通过理论分析和对比实验，探讨VLLM在文本挖掘中的三个优势
AI.result: 指出VLLM在文本挖掘中的三个优势，并识别出方法设计与开发中的挑战
AI.conclusion: VLLM有望成为文本挖掘的统一方法论，但需克服技术与工程挑战

authors_text: Ishaq Aden-Ali,Yeshwanth Cherapanamjeri,Abhishek Shetty,Nikita Zhivotovskiy
title: The One-Inclusion Graph Algorithm is not Always Optimal
title_zh: 一包含图算法并不总是最优的
abstract: The one-inclusion graph algorithm of Haussler, Littlestone, and Warmuth achieves an optimal in-expectation risk bound in the standard PAC classification setup. In one of the first COLT open problems, Warmuth conjectured that this prediction strategy always implies an optimal high probability bound on the risk, and hence is also an optimal PAC algorithm. We refute this conjecture in the strongest sense: for any practically interesting Vapnik-Chervonenkis class, we provide an in-expectation optimal one-inclusion graph algorithm whose high probability risk bound cannot go beyond that implied by Markov's inequality. Our construction of these poorly performing one-inclusion graph algorithms uses Varshamov-Tenengolts error correcting codes. Our negative result has several implications. First, it shows that the same poor high-probability performance is inherited by several recent prediction strategies based on generalizations of the one-inclusion graph algorithm. Second, our analysis shows yet another statistical problem that enjoys an estimator that is provably optimal in expectation via a leave-one-out argument, but fails in the high-probability regime. This discrepancy occurs despite the boundedness of the binary loss for which arguments based on concentration inequalities often provide sharp high probability risk bounds.
summary: The one-inclusion graph algorithm of Haussler, Littlestone, and Warmuth achieves an optimal in-expectation risk bound in the standard PAC classification setup. In one of the first COLT open problems, Warmuth conjectured that this prediction strategy always implies an optimal high probability bound on the risk, and hence is also an optimal PAC algorithm. We refute this conjecture in the strongest sense: for any practically interesting Vapnik-Chervonenkis class, we provide an in-expectation optimal one-inclusion graph algorithm whose high probability risk bound cannot go beyond that implied by Markov's inequality. Our construction of these poorly performing one-inclusion graph algorithms uses Varshamov-Tenengolts error correcting codes. Our negative result has several implications. First, it shows that the same poor high-probability performance is inherited by several recent prediction strategies based on generalizations of the one-inclusion graph algorithm. Second, our analysis shows yet another statistical problem that enjoys an estimator that is provably optimal in expectation via a leave-one-out argument, but fails in the high-probability regime. This discrepancy occurs despite the boundedness of the binary loss for which arguments based on concentration inequalities often provide sharp high probability risk bounds.
abstract_zh: Haussler、Littlestone 和 Warmuth 的单包含图算法在标准 PAC 分类设置中实现了最佳的预期风险界限。在第一个 COLT 开放问题中，Warmuth 推测这种预测策略总是意味着风险的最优高概率界限，因此也是一种最优 PAC 算法。我们以最强烈的方式反驳这一猜想：对于任何实际有趣的 Vapnik-Chervonenkis 类，我们提供了一种符合预期的最优单包含图算法，其高概率风险界限不能超出马尔可夫不等式所暗示的范围。我们构建这些性能不佳的单包含图算法时使用了 Varshamov-Tenengolts 纠错码。我们的负面结果有几个含义。首先，它表明，基于单包含图算法的推广的几种最近的预测策略继承了同样糟糕的高概率性能。其次，我们的分析显示了另一个统计问题，该问题的估计量通过留一法论证在期望中是最优的，但在高概率情况下却失败了。尽管二元损失有界，但基于集中不等式的论证通常会提供尖锐的高概率风险界限，但这种差异还是会发生。

AI.tldr: 本文反驳了Warmuth关于One-Inclusion Graph算法在高概率下始终最优的猜想，证明其高概率风险界无法超越Markov不等式限制。
AI.motivation: 解决COLT会议提出的开放问题，验证One-Inclusion Graph算法是否在高概率下保持PAC最优性。
AI.method: 通过构造基于Varshamov-Tenengolts纠错码的反例，分析算法在高概率下的风险界表现。
AI.result: 证明对于任意实用VC类，存在期望最优但高概率界受限于Markov不等式的One-Inclusion Graph算法，并揭示其对相关预测策略的影响。
AI.conclusion: One-Inclusion Graph算法的高概率性能不足，表明期望最优性不必然导出高概率最优性，需重新评估相关算法的可靠性。

ai_content.motivation: 解决COLT会议提出的开放问题，验证One-Inclusion Graph算法是否在高概率下保持PAC最优性。
ai_content.method: 通过构造基于Varshamov-Tenengolts纠错码的反例，分析算法在高概率下的风险界表现。

search_text: The One-Inclusion Graph Algorithm is not Always Optimal 一包含图算法并不总是最优的 The one-inclusion graph algorithm of Haussler, Littlestone, and Warmuth achieves an optimal in-expectation risk bound in the standard PAC classification setup. In one of the first COLT open problems, Warmuth conjectured that this prediction strategy always implies an optimal high probability bound on the risk, and hence is also an optimal PAC algorithm. We refute this conjecture in the strongest sense: for any practically interesting Vapnik-Chervonenkis class, we provide an in-expectation optimal one-inclusion graph algorithm whose high probability risk bound cannot go beyond that implied by Markov's inequality. Our construction of these poorly performing one-inclusion graph algorithms uses Varshamov-Tenengolts error correcting codes. Our negative result has several implications. First, it shows that the same poor high-probability performance is inherited by several recent prediction strategies based on generalizations of the one-inclusion graph algorithm. Second, our analysis shows yet another statistical problem that enjoys an estimator that is provably optimal in expectation via a leave-one-out argument, but fails in the high-probability regime. This discrepancy occurs despite the boundedness of the binary loss for which arguments based on concentration inequalities often provide sharp high probability risk bounds. Haussler、Littlestone 和 Warmuth 的单包含图算法在标准 PAC 分类设置中实现了最佳的预期风险界限。在第一个 COLT 开放问题中，Warmuth 推测这种预测策略总是意味着风险的最优高概率界限，因此也是一种最优 PAC 算法。我们以最强烈的方式反驳这一猜想：对于任何实际有趣的 Vapnik-Chervonenkis 类，我们提供了一种符合预期的最优单包含图算法，其高概率风险界限不能超出马尔可夫不等式所暗示的范围。我们构建这些性能不佳的单包含图算法时使用了 Varshamov-Tenengolts 纠错码。我们的负面结果有几个含义。首先，它表明，基于单包含图算法的推广的几种最近的预测策略继承了同样糟糕的高概率性能。其次，我们的分析显示了另一个统计问题，该问题的估计量通过留一法论证在期望中是最优的，但在高概率情况下却失败了。尽管二元损失有界，但基于集中不等式的论证通常会提供尖锐的高概率风险界限，但这种差异还是会发生。 本文反驳了Warmuth关于One-Inclusion Graph算法在高概率下始终最优的猜想，证明其高概率风险界无法超越Markov不等式限制。 Ishaq Aden-Ali,Yeshwanth Cherapanamjeri,Abhishek Shetty,Nikita Zhivotovskiy cs.LG,cs.DS,math.ST
